name: Azure OpenAI Service API
what_it_does: Microsoft's enterprise deployment of OpenAI models (GPT-4, GPT-3.5, DALL-E, Whisper, Embeddings) on Azure infrastructure. Same models as OpenAI but with Azure's compliance, data residency, SLAs, and enterprise security.
use_cases: Enterprise AI agent deployment with data residency and compliance requirements
Building LLM-powered applications in organizations already on Azure
Text generation, summarization, and classification in regulated industries
Embeddings for semantic search and RAG pipelines within Azure infrastructure
Image generation with DALL-E in enterprise content workflows
not_for: Quick prototyping without Azure subscription setup
Access to newest OpenAI models before they're deployed to Azure
best_when: An enterprise agent needs OpenAI model capabilities but requires HIPAA compliance, EU data residency, Azure AD integration, or corporate procurement through Microsoft.
avoid_when: You're prototyping quickly, don't need enterprise compliance, or your org doesn't use Azure.

auth.notes: API key for simple access; Azure AD (Entra ID) OAuth2 for production enterprise use. AAD auth enables RBAC, managed identities, and no-credential deployments. Resource endpoint is Azure-specific (not api.openai.com).

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; requires Azure subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Same token-based pricing as OpenAI; GPT-4o ~$5/1M input, $15/1M output tokens
pricing.notes: Pricing mirrors OpenAI's pricing. Requires Azure subscription. Provisioned throughput (PTUs) available for reserved capacity at committed cost. Enterprise agreements may negotiate rates.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by deployment; typically 10-1000 requests/minute depending on tier and quota

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Uses same error format as OpenAI API. Azure-specific errors include deployment and quota errors. Error messages are clear. Rate limit errors include Retry-After header.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LLM completions are not deterministic but the API calls themselves are stateless. No idempotency concerns for generation calls.
known_agent_gotchas: Azure endpoint URL differs from OpenAI — must configure SDK with Azure-specific base URL and API version
Models must be deployed to your Azure resource before use — not available by default
API version parameter is required in all Azure OpenAI requests
Model availability varies by region — not all models available in all Azure regions
Content filters are more aggressive by default than direct OpenAI — may trigger unexpectedly
Quota limits set per deployment and require support ticket to increase
AAD auth requires setting up app registration — more setup than API key
